methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's partnership with AI Ready RVA signals intentional distributed capacity-building through community-focused education infrastructure, potentially seeding peer learning networks that extend beyond traditional tech hubs into broader Richmond Metro populations.

signals.title: ODU and Google launch AI incubator to boost innovation in Hampton Roads - 13newsnow.com
signals.signal_reading: ODU-Google AI incubator concentrates platform and expertise in Hampton Roads but relies on corporate partnership model that may create shallow, tool-dependent adoption rather than deep indigenous capability unless accompanied by foundational skill development.

signals.title: Report says 35% of the jobs in Virginia may be at risk to artificial intelligence. Southside the least impacted, Northern Virginia the most. - Cardinal News
signals.signal_reading: Report finding 35% of Virginia jobs at AI risk with Southside least impacted and NoVA most exposed reveals automation's uneven geography—but 'least impacted' may mean least prepared, signaling neither depth of capability nor breadth of readiness in vulnerable regions.

signals.title: EPA grant backs community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia - Virginia Tech News
signals.signal_reading: EPA-backed community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia demonstrates distributed capacity-building in historically underserved coal-transition region, though success depends on whether training creates deep technical fluency or shallow credential acquisition.

signals.title: AI pressure grows on Richmond's young workforce - Axios
signals.signal_reading: AI pressure on Richmond's young workforce suggests platform-delivered automation is creating shallow adoption patterns where workers must adapt to tools rather than developing deep understanding, potentially accelerating skill displacement without building resilience.
